OPPOSITION TO SPECIAL MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE THE CROSS COMPLAINT OF EMMANUEL DEMOUSSSY FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT DEMOUSSY, EMMANUEL N AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT ARTURO ARAQUE'S NOTICE OF SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE CROSS-COMPLAINT OF EMMANUEL DEMOUSSY IS GRANTED. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS STRICKEN. EACH CLAIM IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ARISES FROM NON- INCIDENTAL PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND MR. DEMOUSSY HAS NOT SHOWN THAT ANY OF THOSE CLAIMS HAVE THE REQUISITE MINIMAL MERIT. THE MOVING PARTY IS ENTITLED TO RECOVER ATTORNEY'S FEE AND COST IN A SUBSEQUENT MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

DEFENDANT MUST CEASE ALL WORK ON PLAINTIFF'S SIDE OF THE PROPERTY LINE AND ALLOW PLAINTIFF WITHIN 2 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER AN OPPORTUNITY TO INSPECT TO DETERMINE WHAT WORK HAS BEEN DONE ON PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY IN ORDER TO DETERMINE COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER. LAW AND MOTION 302, THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION WAS NOT OPPOSED BUT THE AMOUNT OF THE ATTORNEYS' FEE WAS DISCUSSED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF THE HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF ARTURO ARAQUE'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS INCURRED IN BRINGING SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE CROSS-COMPLAINT OF EMMANUEL DEMOUSSY IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED $8000 AS REASONABLE FEES AND COSTS FOR PREVAILING ON THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION AND BRINGING THIS MOTION FOR FEES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Notice Of Motion For Attorneys' Fees And Costs Incurred In Bringing Special Motion To Strike The X-Complaint Of Emmanuel Demoussy
Off Calendar Jul-17-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kenneth Schnur, Esq. of Benjamin Weill & Mazer, Tel. 415.421.0730, appearing for plaintiff and moving party;
Steven Miyake, Esq. of the Law Office of Steven Miyake, Tel. 415.421.4400, appearing for defendant and opposing party.

The granting of the motion was not opposed but the amount of the attorneys' fee was discussed. At the conclusion of the hearing, the court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ARTURO ARAQUE's Motion For Attorneys' Fees And Costs Incurred In Bringing Special Motion To Strike The Cross-Complaint Of Emmanuel Demoussy is granted. Plaintiff is awarded $8000 as reasonable fees and costs for prevailing on the anti-SLAPP motion and bringing this motion for fees. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2012 Continued To Aug-08-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Arturo Araque'S Special Motion To Strike The Cross-Complaint Of Emmanuel Demoussy
Off Calendar May-30-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KENNETH D. SCHNUR OF THE LAW OFFICES OF BENJAMIN, WEILL & MAZER, TEL. NO. 421-0730 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
EMMANUEL DEMOUSSY, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Arturo Araque's Notice of Special Motion To Strike The Cross-Complaint Of Emmanuel Demoussy IS Granted. The cross-complaint is stricken. Each claim in the cross-complaint arises from non- incidental protected activity and Mr. Demoussy has not shown that any of those claims have the requisite minimal merit. The moving party is entitled to recover attorney's fee and cost in a subsequent motion. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)

Off Calendar Apr-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KENNETH D. SCHNUR OF THE LAW OFFICES OF BENJAMIN, WEILL & MAZER, TEL. NO.
421-0730 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Order To Show Cause Regarding Issuance of Prelimianry Injunction - PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT MUST CEASE ALL WORK ON PLAINTIFF'S SIDE OF THE PROPERTY LINE AND ALLOW PLAINTIFF WITHIN 2 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER AN OPPORTUNITY TO INSPECT TO DETERMINE WHAT WORK HAS BEEN DONE ON PLAINTIFF'S PROPERTY IN ORDER TO DETERMINE COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER.
